steps.title: Find the gesture (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a long, low curving line for the cat's back — a sleeping cat curls into a soft mound, so the line rises gently and dips again. This single curve carries the whole pose.

steps.title: Block the big shapes (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch one large rounded oval for the body and a smaller circle for the head, tucked low against the body. The head sits close to the front paws — sleeping cats fold tight.

steps.title: Find the curled tail (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch a long sweeping curve for the tail wrapping around the body, ending near the nose or paws. Let it overlap the body — the tail passes in front of the haunches.

steps.title: Place the paws and ears (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch two small rounded paws tucked under the chin, just barely visible. On top of the head, mark two small triangles for the ears, tilted slightly outward.

steps.title: Refine the silhouette (pencil)
steps.description: Look across your construction — does the back curve feel like a soft cushion or a sharp ridge? Sleeping cats are soft. Erase and redraw any line that feels too angular. Check that the head sits low enough against the body.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the silhouette
steps.description: Switch to ink mode. In one long flowing pass, trace the cat's outer outline — over the ears, around the back, along the tail, under the belly, and up the front. Where the tail crosses the body, stop the body's outline cleanly at the tail's edge.

steps.title: Ink the face details (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, add two small closed eyes as gentle downward curves — the cat is asleep. A tiny triangle for the nose, a short line for the mouth, and a few whisker dots on each cheek. Keep these marks light and minimal.

steps.title: Suggest the fur (ink)
steps.description: Around the ears, the chest, and where the tail wraps, add short tufted marks following the curve of the body. Let lines wrap around the form rather than running flat across it. Keep most of the body quiet — a few well-placed marks suggest fur better than rendering every hair.

steps.title: Anchor with a shadow (ink)
steps.description: Add a flattened shadow shape on the tiles beneath the cat — a soft elongated wedge that follows the body's curve. This grounds the cat instead of leaving it floating on the floor.
